claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 11 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 11 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Someone who lives with the child is already claiming

Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation

Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age group applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit. I actually have two children I'm asking about - Alex who is 11, and Blake who is 15.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! It's great that you're checking your eligibility for both Alex and Blake. Let me assess each child for you.

Since both Alex (11) and Blake (15) are **under 16 years old**, they fall into the same initial category. Let me navigate to the next step for children in this age group.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about each child's work status:

**Are Alex (11) and Blake (15) both working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

This is typically the case for children of their ages, but I just need to confirm. A simple "yes" for both is fine if neither child is working 24+ hours per week.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them work at all - they're still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great, thank you for confirming. Since neither Alex nor Blake are working, they both pass this check. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are Alex and Blake **NOT receiving** any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

This would be quite unusual for children of their ages (11 and 15), so I expect the answer is "no, they don't receive any such benefits" - but I just need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, neither of them receive any benefits themselves.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are Alex and Blake currently doing any apprenticeships?

Since you mentioned they're both still in school, I assume neither is in an apprenticeship - but I just want to confirm this before moving on.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are doing apprenticeships - they're just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming. Since neither Alex nor Blake are in apprenticeships, let me continue to the next step which will look at your situation as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. **Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements?**

You only need to meet **ONE** of these to qualify:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - This is the most common situation
2. **Have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Have pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. **Have pre-settled status** and are actively looking for work
5. **Have pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself and family
6. **Have pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **Have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA or Swiss national
8. **Living abroad as a Crown servant** (e.g., diplomatic staff)

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, it's simply that they normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK - I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, I need to ask a follow-up question:

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include people working for the UK government overseas, such as:
- Diplomatic staff
- Armed forces personnel stationed abroad
- Civil servants posted overseas

If not, could you tell me a bit more about your situation? For example:
- Are you temporarily abroad but normally resident in the UK?
- Do you have any status under the EU Settlement Scheme (settled or pre-settled)?

This will help me determine if you might qualify through any of the other residency paths.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant. I'm just living abroad - I don't normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, you don't appear to meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

Let me confirm: you've indicated that you:
- Don't normally live in the UK
- Are not a Crown servant living abroad
- Don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

Is that correct? If so, I'm afraid this means you wouldn't be eligible for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake, as the claimant must meet one of the residency requirements.

However, if there's anything I've misunderstood about your situation - for example, if you do have any status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or if you're only temporarily abroad - please let me know and we can continue the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - I don't meet any of those requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to deliver this news, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility

### Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

**Reason:** You do not meet the UK residency requirements.

---

### Assessment for Each Child:

| Child | Age | Child Qualifies? | Eligible for Benefit? |
|-------|-----|------------------|----------------------|
| **Alex** | 11 | ✅ Yes | ❌ No |
| **Blake** | 15 | ✅ Yes | ❌ No |

---

### Explanation:

Both Alex and Blake meet all the criteria to be qualifying children for Child Benefit:
- They are both under 16
- Neither is working
- Neither receives benefits in their own right
- Neither is in an apprenticeship

However, **as the claimant, you must meet UK residency requirements** to receive Child Benefit. Since you:
- Don't normally live in the UK
- Are not a Crown servant living abroad
- Don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

...you are unfortunately not eligible to claim.

---

### What You Could Do:

- **If your circumstances change** and you return to live in the UK, you may then be eligible to claim Child Benefit
- **If another person** who does meet the residency requirements is responsible for the children, they may be able to claim instead
- For more information, visit: [GOV.UK - Child Benefit Eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I'm sorry I couldn't bring you better news. If you have any questions or if your circumstances change, please don't hesitate to check your eligibility again.

reasonings: Alex (age 11) is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship - so Alex would qualify as an eligible child. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant does not normally live in the UK, is not a Crown servant, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, the claimant cannot receive Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake (age 15) is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship - so Blake would qualify as an eligible child. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant does not normally live in the UK, is not a Crown servant, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, the claimant cannot receive Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Both Alex and Blake meet all the requirements to be qualifying children for Child Benefit purposes. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements - they do not normally live in the UK, are not a Crown servant living abroad, and do not have any status under the EU Settlement Scheme. As the claimant must meet residency requirements to receive Child Benefit, the claim is ineligible for both children.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
